fundingSummary.totalRaised: $75M (Series A, April 2026); no prior priced/seed round disclosed
fundingSummary.lastKnownValuation: ~$500M post-money (Series A, April 2026; reported by The Information)
fundingSummary.revenueNote: Pre-revenue research lab; ~6-person team in San Francisco. No disclosed ARR/KPIs or revenue as of June 2026 — the company is building foundation models (FDM-1, a video-trained 'computer use' model; previously open-sourced the 'hertz-dev' audio model) and has not commercialized. No public ticker and no public trading multiples exist for the subject (private). Capital is earmarked for compute scaling, FDM-1 development, and hiring.
fundingSummary.ipoReadiness: Very early — pre-revenue research lab, ~6 employees, founded by Galen Mead and Devansh Pandey. An IPO is not on the horizon; the company is at the foundational-research / pre-product stage and years away from any commercialization or public-market candidacy.

freshnessNote: Subject is a PRIVATE, pre-revenue company with no public ticker — there is no market close and no live trading multiple. The valuation shown is a last-round private mark (reported ~$500M post-money, Series A, April 2026), not a market price. Comparable valuations below are also last-round private marks; their dates legitimately differ by company.

rampDemandSignal.note: Ramp Data not applicable: Standard Intelligence is a ~6-person pre-product research lab, not an established B2B vendor with a measurable corporate-card spend footprint, so it does not appear in Ramp's vendor-adoption index.

comps.name: Thinking Machines Lab
comps.note: Closest structural peer: a pre-product, reputation-driven lab (ex-OpenAI CTO). The $12B seed mark stands as its last priced round.

comps.name: Safe Superintelligence (SSI)
comps.note: Another pre-product lab valued on founder reputation; backers include Greenoaks, a16z, Lightspeed, DST, Alphabet, Nvidia.

comps.name: Cognition (Devin)
comps.note: Closest 'agent that uses software' comp — but revenue-generating, unlike the subject's pre-product research stage.

comps.name: Mistral AI
comps.note: Europe's largest-ever venture round; ASML took ~11% fully diluted. A scaled, productized frontier-lab benchmark.

comps.name: Anthropic
comps.lastRaise: $30B Series G (Feb 2026), led by GIC and Coatue
comps.note: Scaled-incumbent ceiling for the category; reportedly in talks May 2026 to raise more at a >$900B valuation.

comps.name: OpenAI
comps.note: Largest private financing in history; the category's top-end benchmark and a direct computer-use (Operator) competitor.
